mechanism_narrative: Rising federal funds rates increase the discount rate for future corporate earnings, mechanically depressing equity valuations and generating negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next month.
failure_condition: Forward 20-day returns show positive mean direction in >55% of periods following a 10bps or larger FEDFUNDS increase over a 5-year walk-forward test.

mechanism_narrative: A monthly unemployment rate increase exceeding 0.3 percentage points signals a sudden deterioration in labor market tightness, triggering immediate risk-off positioning and negative equity returns within one week.
candidate_trigger: UNRATE rises > 0.3pp in a single month
failure_condition: The signal fails to predict negative 5-day returns in >60% of triggered instances during a 3-year rolling evaluation window.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous expansion in real personal consumption expenditures and housing starts indicates broad-based consumer demand strength, which drives corporate revenue growth expectations and produces positive S&P 500 returns over a quarter.
candidate_trigger: RSAFS and HOUST both exceed their 12-month moving averages
failure_condition: Joint positive deviations in RSAFS and HOUST yield negative or flat 60-day forward returns in >50% of cases over a 4-year backtest.

mechanism_narrative: Falling industrial production signals weakening manufacturing output that directly reduces corporate earnings forecasts, generating negative S&P 500 returns over the next month when inflation remains elevated.
failure_condition: Negative INDPRO growth fails to predict negative 20-day returns when CPIAUCSL exceeds 250, with a Rank IC below 0.02 over a 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Steady growth in total nonfarm payrolls reflects expanding economic activity and rising household income, which supports consumer spending and generates positive equity returns over the next week.
failure_condition: Positive PAYEMS month-over-month changes correlate with negative 5-day forward returns in >55% of observations across a 5-year sample.

mechanism_narrative: A federal funds rate level above 4.5% imposes significant financing costs on highly leveraged sectors, triggering portfolio deleveraging and negative S&P 500 returns over the next quarter.
failure_condition: The threshold condition produces positive 60-day forward returns in >60% of triggered periods during a walk-forward validation.

mechanism_narrative: Divergence between rising industrial production and falling real GDP indicates inventory accumulation rather than final demand growth, which signals an impending economic slowdown and negative equity returns over the next month.
failure_condition: The specified divergence pattern yields positive 20-day forward returns in >50% of instances over a 4-year rolling test.

mechanism_narrative: Elevated real consumption during a softening labor market signals sustained demand resilience that buffers corporate revenues, producing positive S&P 500 returns over the next week.
failure_condition: Positive RSAFS growth fails to predict positive 5-day returns when UNRATE exceeds 4.0%, with a hit rate below 45% over a 3-year evaluation.
